Ethylene Yield Percentage.description:
- The Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio is a key performance indicator that measures the relative production levels of propylene and ethylene in a petrochemical plant
- This ratio provides insights into the product mix and helps in understanding the operational efficiency of the plant
Ethylene Yield Percentage.objective: The objective of monitoring the P/E ratio is to align production with market demand and maximize profitability by optimizing the output of these two critical petrochemical products
Ethylene Yield Percentage.comments:
A higher P/E ratio may indicate a favorable market for propylene, while a lower ratio could suggest a need to adjust production strategies to meet changing market demands.
A higher P/E ratio, such as 10:1, means significantly more propylene is produced than ethylene.

Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF).comments:
- MTBF applies to systems and components that can be repaired and put back into service. For non-repairable items, the Mean Time to Failure (MTTF) metric is used instead.
- MTBF is a useful metric, but it doesn't provide information on the cause or severity of failures. Combining MTBF with other KPIs, such as MTTR (Mean Time to Repair KPI), offers a more complete picture of system performance.

Propylene to Ethylene (P/E) Ratio.comments: Integrating processes like olefin cracking or metathesis units can increase the overall propylene yield and thus the P/E ratio.
